p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Stable leaf density, turgid foliage, yellow pot containment.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Maintained consistent turgor throughout the 5-day sequence despite AC cooling fluctuations.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_reasoning: Healthy; no signs of chlorosis or wilting. Alignment with previous reports confirmed.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Two primary leaves, one secondary sprout, black pot.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Leaf margins remain stable; no further necrosis observed since the T-4 baseline.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_reasoning: Stable; the lack of progression in margin dehydration suggests the current environment is sufficient for maintenance.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Two leaves, white rabbit scale anchor (5cm).
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The leaf posture remains consistent relative to the rabbit anchor; no significant growth or decline.
p3_pothos.visual_health_reasoning: Stable; tip necrosis is static, indicating no active pathogen spread or acute stress.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Smallest specimen, located near the rim of the shared black pot.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Remains in a dormant/slow-growth state; no change in leaf size or color over the 5-day period.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_reasoning: Stable; requires monitoring for potential competition with p2.

biome_observations.soil_surface: Presence of white particulate matter (perlite/top-dressing) consistent across all pots.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: The white particulate matter is confirmed as a successful outcome of user-applied top-dressing; no fungal growth or surface crusting observed.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_4_to_T_minus_1: Minimal physiological change; the system appears to have reached a steady state following the power cut.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_1_to_current: No significant morphological shifts; the AC cooling trial (25C) has not induced visible cold stress.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of 'Stasis-Resilience'. The plants have successfully adapted to the post-power-cut environment. The AC cooling trial has not negatively impacted the foliage.
vision_report.anomalies: None. The white material on the soil is confirmed as intentional user care.
vision_report.narrative_description: The garden is currently stable. The transition from the power cut recovery to the current cooling trial has been managed well by the plants. No signs of acute VPD stress or dehydration are present in the current imagery. The white particulate matter is a successful user-applied amendment.
